title: I forgive the years I didn't sleep
subtitle: A short affirmation for releasing blame and resentment about past sleep difficulties.
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 16 of the SLEEP program. Two statements, said in sequence, for releasing the frustration and shame that can remain long after the difficult sleep years.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 16 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to anchor the forgiveness work from the Day 16 tapping practice.

content.text: A short affirmation from Day 16 of the SLEEP program. Two statements for releasing the blame and resentment that the difficult sleep years can leave behind.
I forgive the years I didn't sleep.
My body and I are on the same side now.
Say each statement three times, with a pause between them. They are not joined into one sentence: the pause gives each one room to land. Hand on chest if useful.
Adapted from Day 16 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
